why_winner: A (Viral) leads with the concrete scroll-stopping image in the first 7 words, then lands a quotable reframe ('we're past can it code') that begs to be screenshotted and quote-tweeted. It carries the contrarian idea inside a tighter, more shareable package than C, and is far punchier than B. C is the strongest runner-up for pure reply/debate volume.

playbook_check.hook_in_first_7_words: pass — A: 'Codex opened my app in a browser'
playbook_check.cta_is_question_not_bait: pass — reply ends on 'What are you using?'
playbook_check.follow_reason_landed: pass — A hits HMM + FINALLY (reframes the benchmark)
playbook_check.no_invented_facts: pass — maps to source ('three more prompts', 'opened browser and tested the flow')

runner_up_notes.C: C's 'Generating code is basically solved. Acting on intent is the real frontier.' is the strongest debate bait — keep C in reserve as a quote-tweet follow-up or a separate post. Its framing is already grafted into A's reframe line.
runner_up_notes.B: B's 'I re-explained three times' is the most human detail; could be folded into A if a story variant is wanted.
